content: Always use keyword arguments for functions with more than two parameters. Use dataclasses or Pydantic models for structured data, never raw dicts for domain objects. Use pathlib.Path over os.path. Use 'from __future__ import annotations' for modern type syntax.

Routes must not contain business logic — delegate to services. Services must not import route modules or Pydantic request schemas. Pydantic schemas must not contain behavior beyond validators. ORM models must not contain business logic. Higher layers depend on lower layers only — no circular imports.

name: Backend dependency flow
description: api/routes/ → services/ → adapters/, models/, workers/. api/websocket/ → services/. services/ → adapters/, models/, core/. adapters/ implement ModelAdapter interface per model family. models/ are SQLAlchemy ORM only. schemas/ are Pydantic request/response shapes. core/ holds database, config, events.

content: Never use 'Any' — use 'object' or type narrowing. All function parameters and return types must have type annotations. Use TypedDict for dictionary shapes crossing API boundaries. Use Literal types instead of magic strings. Use Protocol for structural typing. Pydantic models define all API request/response shapes.

SQLite is the only database. All schema changes go through Alembic migrations. Database sessions must use async context managers. Never store large binary data in SQLite — store filesystem paths instead. Metric points should be batch-inserted for performance during training.

name: fastapi-async-setup
description: FastAPI async setup: use asynccontextmanager lifespan for startup (create dirs, init db), async_sessionmaker with expire_on_commit=False for sessions, aiosqlite driver in DATABASE_URL (sqlite+aiosqlite://). All models use Mapped[] typed columns with from __future__ import annotations.

name: sqlalchemy-orm-circular-imports
description: Avoid circular imports in SQLAlchemy ORM models: use TYPE_CHECKING guard for relationship type hints, and string-based relationship() references ('ClassName') so SQLAlchemy resolves at mapper configuration time, not import time.

name: alembic-circular-fk
description: For tables with circular FKs (projects <-> config_versions), write Alembic migration manually with use_alter=True on the deferred FK constraint. Create config_versions first (without the FK that references projects), then projects (with the deferred FK to config_versions), then add the constraint.

content: For flat-layout pyproject.toml (backend/ at root, not src/), must configure [tool.setuptools.packages.find] with where=['.'] and include=['app*'] to avoid 'Multiple top-level packages' error from setuptools autodiscovery.

name: workbench-config-schema
description: WorkbenchConfig Pydantic hierarchy (13 sub-models + top-level) defined in backend/app/schemas/workbench_config.py. Use Literal[] for all enum-like string fields, union types for optional fields (str | None), and from __future__ import annotations at top. All 14 classes match spec.md section 12.3 exactly.

name: fastapi-annotated-depends
description: FastAPI Depends() must use Annotated type alias to satisfy ruff B008: DbSession = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db_session)], then use as plain parameter type annotation in route handlers.
SQLAlchemy 2.x does not export 'Real' at top level. Existing models (run.py, metric_point.py, suggestion.py) had 'from sqlalchemy import Real' causing ImportError.
resolution: Replace Real with Float in all SQLAlchemy model imports.

name: settings-override-pattern
description: Global app settings (SettingsUpdate/GET/PATCH) implemented as in-memory _overrides dict merged with pydantic-settings AppConfig defaults. Persisted to data/settings.json on PATCH. Loaded on startup via _load_persisted_overrides() called in lifespan.

name: config-diff-format
description: Config version diffs stored as JSON in diff_from_prev column. Computed by flattening both YAML dicts with dot-notation keys then comparing. Format: {changed: {key: {old, new}}, added: {key: val}, removed: {key: val}}. ConfigVersionResponse uses field_validator to parse JSON string to dict on read.

name: model-resolve-meta-device
description: Use torch.device('meta') + AutoModelForCausalLM.from_config() to count parameters without loading weights. Fast, no memory allocation. Falls back to zero counts if meta instantiation fails.

name: in-memory-model-dataset-state
description: Model profiles, adapters, activation snapshots, and dataset samples stored in module-level dicts keyed by project_id. Same pattern as settings-override-pattern. Viable for single-user local app.

name: weight-delta-computation
description: compute_weight_deltas in introspection.py loads state dicts from safetensors (glob *.safetensors) or .bin shards, computes L2 norm of (after - before) per parameter key, returns WeightDeltaResponse with layers sorted by l2_norm desc and total_l2_norm as root-sum-square. Route is POST /projects/{id}/models/deltas. CheckpointNotFoundError raised for missing paths.

name: main-py-phase4-integration
description: main.py integration pattern: import runs router (app.api.routes.runs), websocket router (app.api.websocket.handler), and watchdog recovery (app.services.watchdog.recover_stale_runs); include all three in lifespan after create_tables and _load_persisted_overrides
ConfigVersion ORM model uses yaml_blob not yaml_content for the YAML content column.
resolution: Use config_version.yaml_blob in orchestrator._resolve_config_path when writing config to disk.

name: websocket-connection-manager
description: ConnectionManager in stream.py holds per-connection state (_ClientConnection dataclass), bounded asyncio.Queue (256 items) for backpressure, one sender drain task per connection, event_handlers dict keyed by id(websocket) for cleanup. handler.py is thin: accept, connect, message loop, disconnect. resource_update events emitted every 5s via psutil+torch (cuda/mps fallback to 0.0).

name: ai-recommender-pattern
description: RecommendationEngine ABC in services/ai_recommender.py with RuleBasedEngine (7-rule fallback) and CloudLLMEngine (Anthropic SDK + OpenAI). build_engine() selects engine based on settings. suggestion_service.py handles generate (runs engine, stores), accept (applies config diff to YAML, creates config version with source_tag=ai_suggestion, logs decision), reject. Suggestion config_diff stored as JSON string in DB.

name: storage-management-pattern
description: Phase 6 storage system: StorageRecord ORM (category, total_bytes, file_count) with ProjectStorageResponse schema (breakdown, per_run summaries, retention_policy). Policy: keep_last_n (3), always_keep_best_eval, always_keep_final, delete_intermediates_after_completion. Mark artifacts with is_retained=0. storage_manager service: compute storage, apply policy, log to decision_logs. Download via Content-Disposition header.

name: storage-manager-as-artifact-service
description: When artifact CRUD is in the same file scope as storage_manager.py, implement artifact service functions (list_artifacts, get_artifact, get_artifact_download, delete_artifact) directly in storage_manager.py rather than a separate artifact_service.py — routes import from storage_manager.

name: phase6-storage-endpoints
description: Phase 6 storage endpoints: GET /projects/{id}/storage returns ProjectStorageResponse (breakdown by category, per-run summary, retention policy). GET /storage/total returns StorageTotalResponse (total_bytes, per_project dict, project_count). POST /projects/{id}/storage/cleanup runs retention policy. storage_manager.py computes storage on demand and caches in storage_records table. connection_manager was missing from main.py imports — must import from app.api.websocket.stream.

name: auto-analyze-on-completion-hook
description: _auto_analyze_if_enabled in orchestrator.py: after terminal_status == completed, fire-and-forget asyncio.create_task calling suggestion_service.generate_suggestions if ai_assistant.auto_analyze_on_completion is true in the run config YAML. Wrapped in try/except so analysis failure never affects run completion state.
MetricPoint ORM column is metric_value not value
resolution: Changed r.value to r.metric_value in suggestion_service.py _fetch_run_metrics

description: rule_engine _check_memory_limit: metric name gpu_memory_allocated_mb (MB), threshold max_memory_gb*1024*0.90 from config.execution
resolution: Fixed metric lookup to gpu_memory_allocated_mb, pass max_memory_gb from execution config, compare latest_usage_mb <= max_memory_mb*0.90

name: decision-logging-insertion-pattern
description: DecisionLog rows inserted via session.add(DecisionLog(...)) after state changes in services. watchdog.py inserts after _mark_run_failed with separate commit; storage_manager.py inserts inside _apply_retention_for_run after file deletion. Always set: project_id, action_type, actor, target_type, target_id, notes, created_at.

name: trainer-stderr-capture
description: orchestrator._run_trainer_subprocess must read proc.stderr after proc.wait(). subprocess stderr is piped but never consumed by default, causing trainer import errors to be silently discarded. capture error/stage_fail events into local vars before buffering as logs, then use them in the failure branch of _update_run_status.

name: arch-cache-meta-device
description: Architecture responses are cached in _architecture_cache dict keyed by project_id in model_service.py. Cache is populated eagerly at resolve time using build_architecture_from_config (meta-device instantiation — no weight loading) in introspection.py, and lazily on first GET /architecture if the cache is cold. Cache is invalidated at the top of resolve_model before the new resolve runs. This makes architecture requests O(1) after the first resolve even across page navigations.

name: model-resolve-config-writeback
description: resolve_model in model_service must persist model_id and source to the active config YAML after successful HF resolution. Without this, the in-memory profile is set but the config YAML retains model_id: '' — causing HFValidationError in the trainer subprocess at model_resolution stage. Fix: _write_resolved_model_to_config reads active config, patches model section, creates new config version via config_service, updates active version via project_service.

name: dataset-writeback-on-resolve
description: resolve_dataset in dataset_service.py must be async and call _write_resolved_dataset_to_config after setting _profiles. Mirrors _write_resolved_model_to_config pattern from model_service. Route must await the service call and pass session. Without this, dataset_id stays empty in config YAML and trainer crashes with IndexError.

name: device-fallback-in-training-preparation
description: _stage_training_preparation accepts device: str param; before constructing SFTConfig, if mixed_precision is bf16/fp16 and device != cuda, downgrades to no and logs a warning; sets use_cpu=True and fp16/bf16 flags accordingly; device resolved upstream in _stage_environment_validation

name: workbench-callback-step-progress
description: WorkbenchCallback must implement on_step_end (not just on_log) for per-step progress events. on_log only fires every logging_steps steps so progress bar stalls between log intervals; on_step_end fires every step and emits _emit_progress. Also: on_train_begin must emit stage_complete for training_start before entering training_progress stage, or the frontend stage tracker shows training_start stuck in 'running'.

name: subprocess-unbuffered-stdout
description: Trainer subprocess must be started with sys.executable, '-u' flag and PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 env var to ensure stdout lines reach the orchestrator's async reader immediately. Without this, Python block-buffers stdout on the pipe and events arrive in bulk after the process exits rather than in real-time.

name: dataset-splitcounts-recalc-after-cap
description: resolve_dataset must recalculate SplitCounts after capping rows to max_samples. HF _load_huggingface returns full remote split size; without recalc, profile.split_counts.train=51760 vs total_rows=5176 causes frontend trainRatio=1000%. Fix: if train_ratio provided use ratios x total_after_cap; elif cap applied set train=total_after_cap.

name: trainer-initial-progress-event
description: WorkbenchCallback.on_train_begin should emit an initial progress event (step=0, total_steps=max_steps, progress_pct=0.0) so the frontend shows step count immediately when training enters training_progress stage, before the first step completes.

name: watchdog-failure-diagnostics
description: watchdog.py _build_failure_reason composes rich failure messages from os.waitpid exit signal (_SIGNAL_NAMES map), macOS jetsam system log OOM scan, and heartbeat stage/step/metrics. check_run_health marks runs failed with diagnostics when pid is dead or heartbeat stale.

name: dynamic-training-intervals
description: eval_steps, save_steps, logging_steps in TrainingConfig are int | None = None. In _stage_training_preparation, compute total_steps = steps_per_epoch * epochs then dynamic_interval = max(1, total_steps // 10). Use config value if not None, else dynamic_interval. Default config in project_service emits None for these fields.

name: timeline-skip-stage-on-completion
description: When a run reaches a terminal state (completed, failed, cancelled), call _mark_pending_stages_skipped(run_id=run_id) before updating run status. Stages that never triggered (e.g. evaluation/checkpoint_save when eval_steps/save_steps > total_steps) are stored as 'skipped' not 'pending'. Also called in cancel_run. Frontend StageStatus already includes 'skipped' and renders it distinctly.
